name: YT Zero
description: YT Zero is a self-hosted YouTube inbox for people who want subscriptions, not recommendations. It reads public YouTube RSS feeds, stores everything locally in SQLite, and gives you a calm place to sort, schedule, watch, archive and revisit videos from creators you already follow — without a Google account or an API key. It supports profiles, several authentication methods, child profiles with watch-time limits, tags and rules, local playlists, SponsorBlock, and an optional yt-dlp plugin for downloads and offline playback.

notes.text: The app starts with no channels. Add them from Settings > Channels, or import an OPML file or a Google Takeout subscriptions.csv.
Downloads are optional and off by default. Enabling the YT-DLP Integration plugin stores video files under /opt/ytzero/data/downloads — size the disk accordingly.
Configuration lives in /etc/ytzero/ytzero.env. Run 'systemctl restart ytzero' after editing it.
